dublinCore.title: Skeptic or Cynic?
creator: Dan McCreary
Claude
subject: Digital Citizenship
Media Literacy
Critical Thinking
dublinCore.description: An Explore-and-Quiz MicroSim that helps students tell the difference between healthy skeptical phrases (that ask good questions) and unhealthy cynical phrases (that have given up on the truth).

learningObjectives: Distinguish between skeptical phrases (curious, evidence-seeking) and cynical phrases (dismissive, hopeless)
Recognize the kinds of questions a skeptic asks before sharing online information
Identify tone signals that reveal cynicism
prerequisites: Familiarity with the 'pause, think, act' habit from Chapter 2

technical.accessibility: describe() screen reader label, high-contrast colors, large readable text

controls.label: Switch to Quiz Mode / Switch to Explore Mode
controls.purpose: Toggles between the two modes of the MicroSim

controls.label: More...
controls.purpose: Explore mode: pulls five fresh skeptic and five fresh cynic phrases from the bank of twenty of each

controls.label: Skeptic Phrase
controls.purpose: Quiz mode: claims the current phrase is a skeptic phrase

controls.label: Cynic Phrase
controls.purpose: Quiz mode: claims the current phrase is a cynic phrase

controls.label: Next Phrase
controls.purpose: Quiz mode: advances to a new phrase after an answer

controls.label: Reset Score
controls.purpose: Quiz mode: resets the score and ends any celebration animation

assumptions: Phrases are drawn from a fixed bank in phrases.json (20 skeptic, 20 cynic)
Celebration triggers at 10 correct answers in a single run
limitations: Binary classification only. Real-world phrases can be mixed or ambiguous.
